If you redeem this offer and then cancel your paid Slack plan or downgrade to a free plan during the Discount Period, this offer may no longer be available to you.

evidence.page_title: Slack Pricing Plans: Find the Right Fit for Your Team | Slack
evidence.content:
How does Slack protect the security of my data when using AI features?

AI in Slack runs in Slack’s trusted infrastructure, and upholds the [security practices and compliance standards](https://slack.com/trust/data-management/privacy-principles) that customers expect from Slack itself. Slack does not share customer data with large language model (LLM) providers and does not use customer data to train LLMs. Visit our [Help Center](https://slack.com/help/articles/28310650165907-Security-for-AI-features-in-Slack) for more details.

Our large language models are hosted directly within Slack’s AWS virtual private cloud, ensuring your data remains in-house.

How can I pay for Slack? Can I pay by credit card, or can you invoice me?

You can use a credit card to pay for any plan. If you choose to pay annually, you may be eligible to be invoiced and pay by ACH or wire transfer. [Visit our Help Center](https://slack.com/help/articles/360002038947-Supported-payment-methods#pay-by-invoice) for more details, or contact us to start the process.

If you choose to pay by credit card, you’ll see the first credit card charge from Slack on the day you purchase a paid plan. Here are some other times you’ll see charges:

- On your monthly renewal date if you pay monthly.
- On your annual renewal date if you pay annually.
- On the last day of the month if you pay annually and there’s an outstanding balance for adding new members.

You can change billing cycles or switch to invoicing at any time. Learn more about managing billing details [here](https://slack.com/help/articles/218915087-Manage-your-Slack-plan-and-billing-details#change-your-billing-cycle).

Our team has several accounts which are not being actively used, but which we don’t want to disable. Do we still have to pay for them?

At Slack, we believe you should only be billed for what you use. Members who take an action in Slack at any time within a 28-day period will be considered active for billing purposes. If someone you’ve already paid for becomes inactive, we’ll add a prorated credit to your account for the unused time.

We want to add new users to our workspace. How will that be billed?

You’ll be charged a prorated amount for new team members for the remainder of the current billing period. For a detailed overview of our billing model and how your subscription term affects when you will be billed, [visit the Help Center](https://slack.com/help/articles/218915077-Slacks-Fair-Billing-Policy).

Which plan do I need to use Slackbot?

Soon, Slack users across every plan will be able to try Slackbot. Demand has been extraordinary, and we are opening access so every team can experience an agent built directly into their flow of work. Slack Free and Pro users will have the chance to explore Slackbot’s capabilities firsthand through a limited trial experience. Ongoing Slackbot access will continue to be available to Business+ and Enterprise+ customers, and coming soon, Salesforce CRM customers who connect their accounts to Slack will also receive ongoing access to Slackbot!

Do you offer discounts for non-profit organizations or educational institutions?
